item.title: Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel DLC Brings Conan the Barbarian In Big Update
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve been enjoying Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel since its September 2025 launch, then there’s some good news for you: You’ll soon be able to use the fearsome and ferocious Conan the Barbarian as a playable character as part of new DLC in a huge Jotunnslayer update.

Fresh off the press is word that Conan the Barbarian is not content with dominating in the Hyborian Age; the legendary fighter is trying his luck in Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel, fighting deities in endless loops of gameplay.

Conan the Barbarian & Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel Crossover Adds Co-Op

Conan the Barbarian is the standout inclusion in the upcoming Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel update, but it also brings with it a free co-op gameplay addition as well.

Here’s an official press release to explain more:

“In Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel, Conan arrives as a force of pure battlefield dominance. His combat style is defined by momentum and aggression, centered around his unique Triumph mechanic. By defeating enemies, Conan builds Triumph stacks that allow him to shrug off incoming damage, turning him into an unstoppable juggernaut. With the Born in Battle trait granting elevated critical lethality and the Ram Dash allowing him to plow through enemy ranks, Conan is a slayer without equal.”

Conan has three sub-classes, offering three completely unique ways of experiencing him, and the DLC explores his iconic home of Stygia.

Additionally, the update adds the God of Fate, which delivers a compelling ‘high-stakes risk-and-reward blessing system.’

Then there’s the free co-op mode, to wrap up an inviting package:

“Local Co-op launches as a FREE UPDATE.

Built and refined based on your playtest feedback, co-op is designed for shared chaos without slowing down the pace of combat.

Key features:

– two-player shared runs

– alternating level-up choices to keep a fast flow

– expanded build synergy across classes and gods

– adjusted active skill slots to preserve balance

– drop-in assistance – host retains progression

It’s a different tactical experience – more coordination, more combo potential, more battlefield moments.”

Insider Gaming is pleased to confirm that the Conan the Barbarian DLC and co-op update will arrive on April 9 for applicable platforms and consoles: PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S.

Check out the announcement trailer right here.

There are a lot of people playing Jotunnslayer: Hordes of Hel. Are you one of them? Let us know via the Insider Gaming Discord, and say why you’re loving it so much.

Check out the Steam Deck 2 targeting a 2028 release, and Nexon saying The First Descendant has ‘No Staying Power’ for more trending gaming news.

item.title: Famitsu Sales: 3/23/26 – 3/29/26
item.content:encoded:
Famitsu has published its estimated physical game software data for Japan for the week of March 16, 2026 to March 22, 2026.

Pokemon Pokopia for Switch 2 remained the highest selling game of the week, moving an additional 48,772 retail copies.

Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection was the highest selling new release of the week, opening at 18,238 retail copies sold on Switch. The PlayStation 5 version did not chart in the top 10.

Super Mario Bros. Wonder – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Meetup in Bellabel Park debuted at 10,158 retail copies sold.

On the hardware side, Switch 2 sold 65,725 units, the PlayStation 5 family sold 19,404 units, the Switch family sold 18,542 units, the Xbox Series family sold 440 units, and the PlayStation 4 family sold 16 units.

item.title: ICYMI: Donkey Kong Bananza's Newest DLC Event Is Now Available
item.content:encoded:
Banana powered

Donkey Kong Bananza's Emerald Rush DLC kicked off its third event of 2026 earlier this week, and it features a pair of classic Kongs in statue form that long-time fans will want to grab.

'Unyielding Strength' started on 31st March and runs until 7th April 2026 at 7:59am BST / 8:59am CET / 11:59 PT (6th), giving you a chance to grab statues of Wrinkly Kong (who first appeared in Donkey Kong Country 2, though mentioned in the first) and Tiny Kong (from Donkey Kong 64).

The event takes place on DK Island, meaning once you grab those statues, you can put them down immediately without travelling to your lovely little island nook.

Just as a reminder, like previous events, you can only take part in this if you have the DK Island & Emerald Rush DLC, and you'll need to dive into the game's roguelike side to unlock those statues. That means limited perks and upgrades, too.

We keep a record of all current and previous DLC events in our Emerald Rush guide below, so make sure you check that out and keep it bookmarked for future reference!

Want your own Wrinkly and Tiny Kong for your island? Let us know if you're taking part in the event in the comments.

[source x.com]

Alana has been with Nintendo Life since 2022, and while RPGs are her first love, Nintendo is a close second. She enjoys nothing more than overthinking battle strategies, characters, and stories. She also wishes she was a Sega air pirate.

item.title: Call of Duty Veteran Jason Blundell Returns With Another New Studio
item.content:encoded:
Call of Duty veteran Jason Blundell, who hit headlines last week thanks to Sony's closure of his previous studio Dark Outlaw Games, has returned with another new development outfit.

As spotted by the folks at KitGuru, Blundell revealed the existence of his new studio during a conversation with YouTuber and fellow ex-Dark Outlaw staffer JCbackfire. The company's name is Magic Fractal, and we don't know very much about it or what it's up to yet.

Presumably, Blundell will announce more in the coming months, but given that his last studio was closed down by Sony before its first project was even announced, any jitteriness on Blundell's part regarding making another big announcement is entirely understandable.

Blundell also revealed a logo during the conversation with JCbackfire, but it's a fairly innocuous affair, sporting just the company's name and some artfully-arranged retro-style square artwork.

During another stream with JCbackfire last week, Blundell confirmed that the game on which he and Dark Outlaw were working was not a live-service game, despite his extensive experience with the Call of Duty franchise.

As such, it's possible that Blundell, JCbackfire, and the other ex-Dark Outlaw staffers will shift all of the work they've done (however much that may be) on that unannounced project towards whatever Magic Fractal's debut game turns out to be.

However, it's worth noting that the state of the industry right now is volatile, to say the least. Plenty of studios started by starry-eyed industry veterans close down without ever releasing a game, so it's probably best to wait until Blundell and Magic Fractal's first game is a little more concrete before getting excited.

It'd be nice to report, in a few months (or even years), that Blundell finally managed to get a game off the ground after leaving Treyarch, so let's hope that's how things go this time around. We'll bring you more as soon as we get it.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph

item.title: Garry's Mod Spiritual Successor s&box Launches Later This Month
item.content:encoded:
Developer and publisher Facepunch Studios has announced that s&box (say it out loud!), its spiritual successor to Garry's Mod, will be launching later this month.

The release date for s&box was quietly revealed as part of the game's latest patch notes on Steam, which include changes like an overhauled vehicle camera, the ability to save and load play sessions in sandbox mode, and more (and the launch date, of course). The game is coming on April 28th.

Facepunch says it "tried to quietly announce" the release date, but "it seems everyone has got excited", so the attempt to slip under the radar failed, evidently. Prior to launch, Facepunch will revoke s&box from "anyone who hasn't played a lot recently, developed games, or bought skins", after which the game will cost $20.

s&box first entered open beta all the way back in 2021, so it's been in the works for quite some time. At that point, Garry Newman, who, as you've probably figured out, was the driving force behind Garry's Mod, described s&box as an attempt at creating a "worthy" sequel to the creativity tool.

The original Garry's Mod uses the first iteration of the Source engine, but s&box is built on Source 2, the engine that's also been used for games like VR tour de force Half-Life: Alyx and updated shooter Counter-Strike 2 (as well as still-under-wraps hero shooter Deadlock).

The Steam page for s&box describes the project as "a game creation platform" in which you can "play, create, and share games, explore community creations, and experience endless sandbox fun" using "powerful modern tools".

Not only can you make games within s&box, but you can also build cosmetic microtransactions into them, allowing you to earn cash from players checking out your game, Roblox-style. The plan is also to let players publish their games on Steam royalty-free, although Facepunch says it's "not quite ready" for this yet.

If you'd like to check it out for yourself, s&box launches exclusively on PC via Steam on April 28th.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
